utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know the nurse told you about dax .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so ralph is a 62-year-old male with a past medical history significant for depression and prior lobectomy as well as hypertension , who presents for his annual exam . so , ralph , it's been a while since i saw you . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , relatively speaking , okay . it was kind of a , a tough spring with all the pollen and everything and , uh , we dropped my oldest daughter off at college and moved her into her dorm , so little stressful , little chaotic , in the heat of the summer , but so far , so good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i know . i know . that's a , that's a hard thing to get over , moving kids out of the house and that type of thing .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , um well , how are you doing from , you know , let's talk a little bit about your depression . how are you doing with that ? i know that we had put you on the prozac last year .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i've been staying on top of the meds , and i have n't had any incidents in a while , so it's , it's been pretty good , and everything's managed and maintained . um , still kind of working with my hypertension . that's been a little bit more of a struggle than anything .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . yeah , i , i see that we have you on the norvasc . and so are you taking it at home ? is it running high , or ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i ... i'm pretty regular with the medications during the business week , but on there's weekends , you know , if i'm on the fly or doing something , sometimes i forget , or i forget to bring it with me . uh , but for the most part , it's been okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , and then i know that you've had that prior lobectomy a couple years ago . any issues with shortness of breath with all the allergies or anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: other than during the heat and the pollen , it's been pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . so i , i know that the nurse went over the review of systems sheet with you , and , and you endorsed some nasal congestion from the pollen , but how about any shortness of breath , cough , muscle aches ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sometimes i , i regularly , uh , go for a run in the morning . that's my workout , and sometimes if it's , uh , relatively humid , i'll struggle a little bit , and i might feel a little bit of pounding in my chest . it usually goes away , but , uh , again , for the most part , it's been pretty good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so you also have some shortness of breath with with exertion .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: correct . correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , and how far are you running ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , like 4 to 5 miles a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . all right . well , let's go ahead . i'd like to do a quick physical exam . let's look at your blood pressure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so here in the office today , your blood pressure looks quite well , at 120 over 80 . let's look at your prior trends . hey , dragon , show me the blood pressure readings . so , yeah , it looks , it looks good . i think you're doing a good job . it looks lower than it has in the past , so continue on the current medication .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so i'm just gon na listen to your heart and lungs and check you out , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so on exam , everything seems to be good . your heart , i hear a slight two out of six systolic ejection murmur , and your lungs sound nice and clear , and you do n't have any lower extremity edema . um , your ... you do have some pain to palpation of the , of the sinuses here , so i think you do have a little bit of congestion there . let's go ahead and look at some of your results , okay ? hey , dragon , show me the ekg . so they did an ekg before you came in today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and in reviewing the results , it looks like your ekg is completely normal , so that's good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i'm not too concerned about that , that chest pounding . hey , dragon , show me the chest x-ray . and we also did a chest x-ray , which , which looks really good , uh , and you know , your prior lobectomy , there's no ... everything looks good , okay ? it looks normal . so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you . so for your first problem , your , your depression , it seems , again , like you're doing really well-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . with your current strategy . let's continue you on the prozac 20 milligrams a day and do you need a refill on that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , actually , i do need a refill .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , order a refill of prozac , 20 milligrams daily . from a ... for your next problem , the lobectomy , i think , you know , i do n't think we need to do any more workup of that . it seems like you're exercising a lot . your breathing function is fine . so , uh , i , i do n't think you need to follow up with the surgeon anymore . and then for your last problem , your hypertension .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're doing a great job of keeping it controlled . i know you said you have n't been taking it that much on the weekends , but your blood pressure here looks good , and it's much better over the last several years . so let's go ahead . i do wan na order just , um , an echocardiogram for that murmur . hey , dragon , order an echocardiogram . and i'll just follow up with the results , and we'll go ahead and order , um , your routine blood work , and i'll be in touch with you through the patient portal , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: perfect .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: same here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note . the nurse will be in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 62-year-old male with a history of depression, prior lobectomy, and hypertension presenting for an annual exam.
The patient moved his daughter into college over the summer, which was stressful.
Depression
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with his depression.
The patient reports consistency with his medication and no recent incidents of depression.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient is taking his Norvasc and if his blood pressure is running high.
The patient reports taking Norvasc regularly during the business week but occasionally forgetting it on weekends.
Lobectomy
The doctor asks about shortness of breath related to allergies.
The patient reports breathing is generally good except during heat and pollen exposure.
The patient endorses nasal congestion from pollen.
The doctor asks about shortness of breath, cough, and muscle aches.
The patient runs 4 to 5 miles every morning.
The patient reports struggling with breathing and chest pounding during humid runs.
The patient confirms dyspnea on exertion.
The doctor asks how far the patient runs.
The patient runs 4 to 5 miles a day.
The patient's blood pressure is 124/80 mmHg.
Physical exam reveals a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, no edema, and sinus tenderness.
The EKG is normal.
The chest X-ray is unremarkable.
Prozac 20 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient needs a refill for Prozac.
The patient confirms he needs a refill for Prozac.
Prozac 20 mg daily.
The doctor discontinues surgical follow-up for the lobectomy.
The doctor orders an echocardiogram and routine blood work, with follow-up via the patient portal.
